title: Memory, Storage, RAID, and Removable Media · Support Decision Defense
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for memory, storage, raid, and removable media and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for memory and storage integrity decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare RAM generation, form factor, capacity, channels, speed, timing, voltage, ECC, registered status, compatibility, and population rules.
Distinguish capacity pressure, application faults, thermal instability, compatibility, contact, and hardware errors using repeatable evidence.
Compare magnetic, flash, optical, removable, SATA, SAS, NVMe, form factor, interface, endurance, latency, throughput, and workload fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Storage Health Custody
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The quiz variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Storage Health Custody.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for preserve data custody while assessing capacity, smart or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition. For CompTIA A+ support Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Storage Health Custody, keep Storage Health Custody connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Storage Health Custody
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Storage Health Custody: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The quiz variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Storage Health Custody.
steps.why: Retrieving Storage Health Custody through the concrete memory and storage integrity decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Storage Health Custody, keep Storage Health Custody connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Storage Health Custody, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Storage Health Custody precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Storage Health Custody, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Crashes, storage pressure, a degraded array, and a healthy-looking drive blur capacity, hardware, filesystem, redundancy, backup, and custody boundaries. The quiz variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Preserve data custody while assessing capacity, SMART or vendor health, filesystem state, encryption, backups, safe removal, replacement, and disposition.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Storage Health Custody, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A drive health percentage is a complete diagnosis and exact lifetime forecast.
